metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_4541
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Figure 1A: SDS-digested freeze-fracture replica labelling (SDS-FRL) with an antibody specific for the Cacna1a subunit of the Cav2.1 P/Q-type VGCCs shows immunogold particles (5 nm diameter) for Cav2.1 are predominantly found on the presynaptic P-face, with little labelling on the exoplasmic face or cross-fractured face.
Figure S1A-B: Specificity of the antibody in replica labelling was confirmed using Cav2.1 knockout mice which showed little Cav2.1 labelling.

Figure S2C-D: Cav2.1 labelling followed by RIM staining revealed that the majority of Cav2.1 clusters are closely associated with RIM particles supporting that Cav2.1 are localized in AZs.

The term "integral component.." was chosen based on the transmembrane topology of the Cacna1a subunit (P54282_RAT)

Note: Reference tracking shows that the antibody used in the study is specific against the Cacna1a subunit of the Cav2.1 channel (PMID: 22279216).. Experimental description: P7-P21 rats and mice were perfused with fixative, and brain slices were frozen, fractured, and replicated as described previously (Indriati et al., 2013). Replicas were incubated with an antibody against Cav2.1 subunit of Ca2+ channel (8.1 μg/ml; Miyazaki et al., 2012) overnight followed by incubation with 5 nm gold-conjugated secondary antibody at 15°C. Identification of 5 nm particles on the replicas was based on their size, round shape, and electron density.
